key_insight: A fourth-order 3D singularly perturbed PDE is split into simpler second-order subproblems plus a Stokes-type system, enabling a robust low-order mixed finite element solution.
engineering_relevance: This makes high-order, parameter-sensitive mechanics and diffusion-type simulations more practical by using standard linear elements and a stable MINI-based mixed discretization.
method: Use a generalized Helmholtz decomposition to decouple the fourth-order problem.
Solve two second-order elliptic equations with standard linear finite elements.
Discretize the remaining curl-free Stokes-type system with the MINI element and an added L2 Lagrange-multiplier term for robustness.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments and a uniform error estimate of order h^{1/2} with respect to the perturbation parameter.
watch_item: The proven accuracy is only first-order-half, so performance may be limited on coarse meshes or when sharper resolution is needed near boundary/interior layers.
paper_title: A Decoupled Low-Order Conforming Mixed Finite Element Method for a Three-Dimensional Fourth-Order Singularly Perturbed Problem
